results.name: Login page renders
results.reasoning: The login page displays correctly with the expected split-screen design. Left panel shows dark-themed branding with 'Sportsphere' logo, 'The Sports Community Built for Athletes' headline, feature list, and live stats. Right panel contains functional login form with email/password fields, Sign In button, and Create Account option. All elements are properly rendered and the page appears fully loaded without errors.

results.name: Invalid credentials rejected
results.reasoning: L2 FAIL: No error message or red toast notification is visible after attempting to sign in with fake credentials. The user appears to still be on the login page with the same form state, but there should be a clear error indication for invalid login attempts.

results.name: Valid login redirects to Feed
results.reasoning: The authenticated app is working correctly. The left sidebar shows all navigation items (Feed, Explore, Reels, ProPath, Live, Messages, Profile, Create Post, More), the main content area displays a functional feed with real user posts including Marcus Silva's soccer post with image and engagement options, story carousel at top with multiple users, post creation prompt, sport category filters (For You, Following, All, Basketball, Soccer, Football), and a Sports News section on the right. All elements are properly loaded with real data and the dark theme is applied correctly.

results.name: Session persists on refresh
results.reasoning: The authenticated feed page is properly displayed after refresh. Shows logged-in user (T) in header, functional sidebar navigation with Feed selected, active story carousel with real user profiles, sport category filters (For You, Following, All, Basketball, Soccer, Football), and a real post from Marcus Silva with image content. The SPORTS NEWS section is visible on the right. User remains authenticated and was not redirected to login/onboarding.

results.name: Sign-up form has role selection
results.reasoning: The sign-up form is working correctly with the 'Create Account' tab active (highlighted in white). All 4 role selection cards are visible (Athlete, Coach, Organization, Parent) with proper icons, descriptions, and interactive styling. The form shows real data including statistics (12K+ active athletes, 340 live streams, 890+ coaches) and a live stream example with viewer count. The layout is properly functioning in the dark theme.

results.name: Logout actually logs out
results.reasoning: Landing page displays correctly with dark theme, shows Sportsphere branding, tagline, description text, and proper CTA buttons (Get Started/Sign In). No authenticated content is visible, which is correct for a logged-out state. Layout is clean and functional.

results.name: Auth guard blocks Feed when logged out
results.reasoning: Landing page displays correctly with proper dark theme, Sportsphere branding, clear value proposition, functional CTA buttons (Get Started/Sign In), and copyright footer. Auth guard is working as expected by showing landing page to unauthenticated users instead of protected feed content.

results.name: Sidebar renders all primary nav
results.reasoning: Left sidebar displays all 7 navigation items (Feed, Explore, Reels, ProPath, Live, Messages, Profile) with proper icons and labels. Feed is correctly highlighted in red as the active item, indicating proper navigation state management.

results.name: Navigate to Explore
results.reasoning: The Explore page is functioning properly with real content loaded. Shows sport category filter pills (All, Baseball, Basketball, Football, Soccer, Tennis, Softball), a functional search bar, and two content sections: 'TRAINING & DRILLS' with 5 video thumbnails showing titles, durations, tags, and engagement metrics, plus a 'POPULAR POSTS' section beginning to load. All elements display actual data rather than placeholder content or loading states.

This indicates the feature is not working properly as it should display a grid of actual video content.

results.reasoning: Feed is properly displaying real content with user Marcus Silva's post about soccer including actual sports-related text 'Every touch matters. Every rep builds muscle memory', a soccer-themed image, timestamp '14 hours ago', proper categorization (Soccer tag), and interaction elements (Follow button). The stories section shows multiple user profiles with names and avatars. The layout is complete with working navigation, sports category filters, and proper dark theme styling.

results.name: Sport filter pills exist
results.reasoning: The sport filter pills are visible and functional - showing 'For You', 'Following', 'All', 'Basketball', 'Soccer', and 'Football' options with proper styling and appropriate icons. The pills appear to be in a scrollable horizontal layout as expected for a sports social media app.

results.name: Sport filter actually filters
results.reasoning: Basketball filter is working correctly - the Basketball pill is highlighted in red/orange showing it's selected, while other sport pills (Soccer, Football) are in darker gray indicating they're not active. The visible post from Jordan Williams is properly tagged with 'Basketball' and shows basketball-related content (gym/training post). The filter is successfully showing only basketball-related content as expected.

results.name: Clear filter restores all posts
results.reasoning: The feed shows mixed sports content with posts tagged as Soccer, and the sports news sidebar displays multiple different sports (football, baseball, etc.), confirming the filter has been cleared and various sports content is visible
